(e) According to the question,
Z = C
B < A = N
C < B
On combining the statements (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
Z = C< B< A = N
Conclusions I. Z < B (true)
II. N > Z (true)
So, it is clear that both Conclusions I and II are true.

(d) According to the question,
T < V = W
X ≥ Y
W > X
On combining statements (i), (ii) and (iii) we get
T < V = W > X ≥ Y
Conclusions I. V > Y (true)
II. V < X (false)
So, it is clear that only Conclusion I is true.

(b) According to the question,
J ≥ K > P = R < N = S
Conclusions I. S ≥ P (false)
II. J > R (true)
So, it is clear that only Conclusion I is true.

(b) Let us check all the options one by one.
From option (a),
33 + 11 – 3 × 6 = 115 ⇒ 33 + 11 – 18 = 115
⇒ 44 – 18 = 115 ⇒ 26 = 115
∴ LHS ≠ RHS
So, option (a) is incorrect.
From option (b),
33 × 11 + 3 – 6 = 115 ⇒ 33 × 11/3 – 6 = 115
⇒ 121 – 6 = 115 ⇒ 115 = 115
∴ LHS = RHS
So, option (b) is correct and there is no need to check other options.

(c) Let us check all the options one by one.
From option (a),
8 2 + 3 5 ÷ 5 5 = 2
↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓
6 + 4 × 8 ÷ 8 8
⇒ 52 + 38 – 88 = 2 ⇒ 90 – 88 = 2
⇒ 2 = 2 ⇒ LHS = RHS
Clearly, option (a) is correct and hence there is no need to check remaining options.

(c) Let us check all the options one by one.
From option (a), 2 3 ÷ 1 7 × 1 3 = 1241
↓ ↓ ↓ ↓
7 × 3 + 3 7
⇒ 27 × 13 + 37 = 1241
⇒ 351 + 37 = 1241
⇒ 388 = 1241 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS
So, option (a) is incorrect.
From option (b),
3 7 ÷ 1 3 × 12 = 112
↓ ↓ ↓ ↓
7 3 × 3 7 +
⇒ 73 × 37 + 12 = 112 ⇒ 2701 + 12 = 12
⇒ 2713 = 112 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS
So, option (b) is incorrect.
From option (c),
23 × 17 + 3 7 = 428
↓ ↓ ↓
7 + 3 × 7 3
⇒ 27 + 13 × 73 = 428 ⇒ 27 + 949 = 428
⇒ 956 = 428 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS
So, Option (c) is incorrect.
From option (d), 3 + 17 × 7 3 = 388
↓ ↓ ↓ ↓
7 × 3 + 3 7
⇒ 27 × 13 + 37 = 388 ⇒ 351 + 37 = 388
⇒ 388 = 388 ⇒ LHS = RHS
So, Option (d) is correct.

(c) Let us check all the options one by one.
From option (a),
15 + 24 ÷ 3 – 6 = 17
⇒ 15 + 8 – 6 = 17
⇒ 23 – 6 = 17
⇒ 17 = 17
∴ LHS = RHS
So, option (a) is correct and hence, there is no need to check other options.

(b) After replacing the symbol
P > L > A > N = T
Hence, P > A and T < L is definitely true.

(c) After placing the symbols,
B > L = D = N ≥ D
Hence, B > N and D ≤ L, is definitely true.

(e) After placing the letters,
P ≤ N < A ≥ L
Hence, A < P is false.

(c) After placing the symbols,
F < O = U = N < D
Hence, F > N and U> D is definitely false.
